comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "Hardening landed on main: change 326 (commit 9573ddf6a) carries the 'rebar-ticket: 3cf7-56db-cbf2-4ba4' trailer and merged via Gerrit (LLM-Review + Verified green). Force-closing due to the SAME commit-gate false-negative this ticket's sibling work addresses: _referencing_commit_exists (transition_close.py:32) runs 'git log' without --all against repo_root, which resolves via the tracker symlink to the main checkout — currently parked by a parallel session on a stale branch that doesn't contain the commit (the commit IS on origin/main). Verified locally: the wait-for-PyPI step detects a served version and the idempotent publish pre-check treats an already-registered version as a no-op success (stubbed publisher never invoked). The permanent fix (scan --all) is proposed and pending approval.". Session: 9573ddf6a.

compiled_state.description:
## What
The `mcp_registry` job in `.github/workflows/release.yml` failed on the v0.7.1 tag
(Actions run 28806482655): the MCP Registry validates that the PyPI package exists,
and returned `PyPI package 'nava-rebar' not found (status: 404)` because it ran in
the seconds right after the PyPI publish, before PyPI served the new version. A
manual re-run of the failed job succeeded once PyPI had propagated.

## Why
A release must not require a human to notice the failure and re-run the job. The job
should absorb the propagation lag itself.

## Fix (in `.github/workflows/release.yml`, `mcp_registry` job)
- Before publishing, **poll PyPI** for the tagged version
  (`https://pypi.org/pypi/nava-rebar/<version>/json` until HTTP 200, bounded ~5 min)
  so the registry's package-ownership check resolves against a live version.
- Wrap `mcp-publisher publish` in a **bounded retry** (a few attempts with backoff)
  that is idempotency-safe: on a failed attempt, if the version is already present in
  the registry, treat it as success (so a retry after a partial publish does not fail).
- Derive the version from the tag (`${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/v}` / `github.ref_name`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `mcp_registry` waits for PyPI to serve the tagged version before `mcp-publisher publish`.
- [ ] `mcp-publisher publish` is retried on failure with a bounded number of attempts and backoff.
- [ ] The retry treats an already-published version (present in the registry) as success.
- [ ] The workflow YAML parses (validated with a YAML loader).
- [ ] The change lands on main through Gerrit (LLM-Review + Verified green).

file_impact.reason: harden mcp_registry: wait for PyPI + idempotent publish retry

compiled_state.title: release.yml: harden mcp_registry against PyPI-propagation race (wait + idempotent retry)
